evidence_quotes.quote: I think there should also be a big concern over video games and how companies removing video games/shutting down servers and not allowing people to play what they have paid for.
The current way is that at any moment they can simply take this product that was fully paid for away and it's not fair.

paragraphs.text: I agree with the policies being addressed in the current papers, but I think there should also be a big concern over video games and how companies removing video games/shutting down servers and not allowing people to play what they have paid for. I know this has been mentioned in the feedbacks already but it is an unfair thing for these companies yo do especially when we know that they can allow players yo host peer yo peer servers off their devices, the biggest example I can think of is the PlayStation 3 as games can be played even to this day on it with no server required from the company so it has been done and yet they refuse to implement anything similar or equivalent to this. Video game companies do this just to force customers to buy a similar product and then repeat. Regardless it's an issue that I believe should be addressed as it is simply taking away something that was paid for (most of the time for a high price). I do not think that companies should be allowed to lease video games that have been paid full price for. The current way is that at any moment they can simply take this product that was fully paid for away and it's not fair. I am posting this publicly as I believe this is to show how I and many others are willing to put our necks out for this issue and so I hope this is added to this legislation. ~Thank you for listening
